Patch Set 9:

Shaun, why are these patches all in a series?  Can't they mostly be separated out and based on tip of master?  It's just much harder to land a series like this...

Patch Set 9:

> Shaun, why are these patches all in a series?  Can't they mostly be
 > separated out and based on tip of master?  It's just much harder to
 > land a series like this...

The reason is very likely that having all of the patches in a single series is the only way for the "end product" (SLES15sp5 server support) to build.

In some cases it is possible to land an "independent" patch from the middle of a series directly to master, if there are no conflicts (either context or semantic).  However, since the patch was not built and tested that way, I don't think Oleg likes to do that.

Patch Set 9:

> Patch Set 9:
> 
> > Shaun, why are these patches all in a series?  Can't they mostly be
>  > separated out and based on tip of master?  It's just much harder to
>  > land a series like this...
> 
> The reason is very likely that having all of the patches in a single series is the only way for the "end product" (SLES15sp5 server support) to build.
> 
> In some cases it is possible to land an "independent" patch from the middle of a series directly to master, if there are no conflicts (either context or semantic).  However, since the patch was not built and tested that way, I don't think Oleg likes to do that.

While experimenting with LU-16577 I accidentally pushed the whole series ...

The ldiskfs: patches should be dropped from this series as they will block landing of client support. The SUSE 15 SP5 + timer_delete should be rebased on master, being a soon-to-be released distro it would be good to have support ready.
Patch Set 9: Code-Review+2
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as 068e5f13fb94802ced68712ee11e7f9cb106d0ae by Oleg Drokin

parents.subject: LU-16509 lnet: quash memcpy WARN_ONCE false positive

commit.subject: LU-13743 build: Explicitly require encryption support
commit.message:
LU-13743 build: Explicitly require encryption support

Linux commit v5.18-rc5-17-gb1241c8eb977
  ext4: move ext4 crypto code to its own file crypto.c

Update the ldiskfs Makefile to exclude crypto.c when
CONFIG_FS_ENCRYPTION is not enabled.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Shaun Tancheff <shaun.tancheff@hpe.com>
Change-Id: Ic8a40f3d395286bb52ed20693fd7cc4755b10556
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/39243
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
